title: Data Exploration & Exploratory Data Analysis
description:
Objective

Master the fundamentals of data manipulation, cleaning, and exploratory data analysis (EDA) using Python libraries such as Pandas and NumPy to uncover initial dataset insights.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Python for Data Science (NumPy, Pandas)
  • Data Cleaning and Preprocessing
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Handling missing values and outliers
  • Data aggregation and group-by operations
Learning Resources:
  • Pandas Documentation – https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Exploratory Data Analysis Dashboard

Load a raw real-world dataset into a Pandas DataFrame, clean missing values, handle categorical variables, and write scripts to compute statistical summaries.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Jupyter Notebook containing clean EDA code and visual plots.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain proficiency in loading, cleaning, and extracting critical insights from raw datasets using advanced Python data structures.

title: Data Visualization & Feature Engineering
description:
Objective

Learn to tell compelling stories with data using advanced plotting libraries and prepare feature sets optimized for machine learning algorithms.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Data Visualization (Matplotlib, Seaborn)
  • Feature Engineering and Scaling
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Correlation matrices and distribution plots
  • Normalization, standardization, and encoding techniques
Learning Resources:
  • Seaborn Gallery – https://seaborn.pydata.org/examples/index.html

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Feature Engineering Pipeline

Build multivariate plots to check feature correlations, implement custom transformation pipelines, and scale features for predictive performance.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Visual representation report featuring distribution and correlation charts.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Master graphical visualization techniques and transformation pipelines to convert raw attributes into high-quality features.

title: SQL & Relational Databases for Data Science
description:
Objective

Learn how to query relational databases, perform complex joins, and extract datasets efficiently using SQL for data analysis workflows.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • SQL Basics and Advanced Queries
  • Connecting SQL Databases with Python
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Joins, aggregations, and subqueries
  • Using SQLAlchemy and SQLite/PostgreSQL with Pandas
Learning Resources:
  • PostgreSQL Tutorial – https://www.postgresql.org/docs/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Relational Database Query Engine

Write complex SQL scripts to extract filtered datasets from a relational database, and integrate database queries directly into a Python data processing pipeline.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • SQL script file containing structured analytical queries.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Develop strong database management and querying skills required to source and manipulate enterprise-scale data.

title: Supervised Machine Learning: Regression & Classification
description:
Objective

Understand and implement core supervised machine learning algorithms for regression and classification tasks using Scikit-Learn.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Regression and Classification Algorithms
  • Model Evaluation and Hyperparameter Tuning
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Linear/Logistic Regression and Random Forest
  • Cross-validation, precision, recall, and ROC-AUC metrics
Learning Resources:
  • Scikit-Learn User Guide – https://scikit-learn.org/stable/user_guide.html

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Predictive Modeling Engine

Train multiple predictive models, optimize hyperparameters using GridSearch, and evaluate their performances using validation metrics.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Trained model file (.pkl or .joblib) with validation accuracy records.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Acquire practical expertise in building, fine-tuning, and evaluating machine learning models to solve business challenges.

title: Advanced Supervised Learning & Ensemble Methods
description:
Objective

Explore advanced ensemble learning techniques such as Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, and Stacking to maximize prediction accuracy.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Gradient Boosting Machines (GBM, XGBoost, LightGBM)
  • Voting and Stacking Classifiers
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Boosting concepts and residual reduction
  • Handling class imbalance with SMOTE and custom loss functions
Learning Resources:
  • XGBoost Documentation – https://xgboost.readthedocs.io/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: High-Performance Ensemble Classifier

Implement an XGBoost pipeline, tackle data imbalance using SMOTE, and tune hyperparameters for top-tier evaluation scores.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Advanced model performance metrics comparison chart.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain mastery over state-of-the-art tree-based algorithms and boosting frameworks utilized in professional data science competitions.

title: Unsupervised Learning & Clustering Techniques
description:
Objective

Explore unsupervised machine learning methods to discover hidden patterns, segment data points, and reduce dataset dimensionality.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Clustering Algorithms (K-Means, DBSCAN)
  • Dimensionality Reduction (PCA)
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Elbow method and silhouette score analysis
  • Feature projection and variance preservation
Learning Resources:
  • Scikit-Learn Clustering Documentation – https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/clustering.html

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Customer Segmentation Engine

Apply K-Means clustering and PCA to segment a customer dataset into targeted behavioral groups, visualizing cluster profiles.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Clustering evaluation report and visualization plots.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Understand how to derive actionable groupings and compress feature spaces from unlabeled data distributions.

title: Natural Language Processing (NLP) & Text Analytics
description:
Objective

Learn text preprocessing, vectorization techniques, and build classification models to analyze textual data and sentiment.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Text Cleaning and Tokenization
  • TF-IDF and Word Embeddings
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Stopword removal, stemming, and lemmatization
  • Bag-of-Words and TF-IDF transformation
Learning Resources:
  • NLTK Documentation – https://www.nltk.org/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Sentiment Analysis Classifier

Clean raw text documents, apply TF-IDF vectorization, and train a machine learning classifier to detect positive versus negative sentiment.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • NLP pipeline script and classification accuracy metrics report.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain competence in processing textual data and building foundational natural language understanding pipelines.

title: Time Series Analysis & Forecasting
description:
Objective

Master time series manipulation, trend decomposition, and forecasting models to analyze temporal data structures.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Time Series Resampling and Rolling Statistics
  • ARIMA and Exponential Smoothing Models
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Stationarity, autocorrelation, and seasonality
  • Trend and residual decomposition
Learning Resources:
  • Statsmodels Documentation – https://www.statsmodels.org/stable/index.html

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Time Series Forecasting Model

Process temporal datasets, check stationarity using ADF tests, and fit an ARIMA forecasting model to predict future values.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Forecasting visualization charts and error analysis report.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain the skills needed to analyze temporal trends and forecast future trends using statistical modeling.

title: Introduction to Deep Learning with TensorFlow & Keras
description:
Objective

Understand neural network architectures, build multi-layer perceptrons, and train deep learning models using TensorFlow and Keras.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Neural Network Fundamentals (Dense Layers, Activations)
  • TensorFlow and Keras Framework
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Forward propagation, backpropagation, and loss functions
  • Overfitting prevention via dropout and regularizations
Learning Resources:
  • TensorFlow Tutorials – https://www.tensorflow.org/tutorials

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Neural Network Classification Model

Design and train a deep multi-layer perceptron using Keras to solve a complex tabular classification task with optimized network layers.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • TensorFlow model checkpoint file and loss/accuracy training history plots.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Acquire foundational knowledge of deep neural networks and practical training mechanics using modern frameworks.

title: Big Data Handling & Spark Introduction
description:
Objective

Learn how to manage and analyze large-scale datasets that exceed single-machine memory limits using PySpark.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Distributed Computing Concepts
  • PySpark DataFrames and SQL Engine
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Resilient Distributed Datasets (RDDs) and transformations
  • Distributed data filtering, aggregation, and joins
Learning Resources:
  • PySpark Documentation – https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/api/python/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Big Data Processing Script

Initialize a Spark session, ingest a large dataset, and run distributed transformations and aggregations to extract summary metrics.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • PySpark processing script with execution validation output.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Understand scalable data processing pipelines and harness distributed computing frameworks for big data operations.

title: Model Interpretation & Production Pipelines
description:
Objective

Learn model explainability techniques using SHAP and LIME, and build robust MLOps inference pipelines.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • Model Explainability (SHAP, LIME)
  • Production Pipelines and Serialization
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Feature importance interpretation and black-box explanations
  • Building reusable Scikit-Learn transformers and deployment artifacts
Learning Resources:
  • SHAP Documentation – https://shap.readthedocs.io/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Model Interpretability Dashboard

Generate SHAP summary and force plots for a trained model to explain individual and global predictions clearly.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Explainability plots and serialized production pipeline artifact.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Gain the ability to interpret machine learning predictions transparently and prepare code artifacts for real-world production systems.

title: Capstone Project & Live Web App Deployment
description:
Objective

Integrate all learned concepts into a complete end-to-end data science capstone project deployed live via a Streamlit web interface.

Learning Phase

1. Main Topics
  • End-to-End Pipeline Integration
  • Streamlit Web App Development
2. Detailed Concepts
  • Model serialization and caching in Streamlit
  • Designing intuitive user input widgets and visualizations
Learning Resources:
  • Streamlit Documentation – https://docs.streamlit.io/

Practical Implementation

Project Task: Capstone Machine Learning Web Application

Build, package, and deploy a fully functional machine learning application allowing users to interact with your trained model live.

Deliverables

  • Public GitHub Repository URL & Live Streamlit / Demo URL (MANDATORY / MUST)
  • Fully functional and deployed web application link.
  • Detailed README explaining setup commands.

Learning Outcomes

Successfully bridge the gap between data science modeling and user accessibility by deploying a production-grade data app.
